Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About AXA

AXA is a global leader in insurance and financial services, dedicated to helping customers protect what matters most to them. As the sixth-largest insurance company in the world, we provide a wide range of services, including health, car, home, and business insurance. We support millions of customers worldwide, helping them navigate life's uncertainties with confidence.

AXA Commercial protects businesses, from multinationals to micro start-ups, giving them the confidence to thrive.

Job Overview

Are you passionate about turning big data into actionable insights? Join our Commercial Pricing Data Team at AXA, where you’ll play a key role in shaping our strategic data landscape. Working closely with colleagues across different teams, you’ll help build a robust, insightful, and accessible data environment that empowers our Pricing Analysts to make informed decisions. If you’re eager to make a real impact through data and enjoy collaborative, innovative work, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.

Key Responsibilities


Work with a variety of data tools and databases to design solutions, as well as maintaining, creating, updating, reading, and deleting data as needed.
Collect data from multiple sources, handling raw data, cleansing it, and converting it into structured formats suitable for further analysis and data science activities.
Develop and maintain a business glossary and data dictionaries, and classifying data in accordance with local priorities.
Collaborate with Data Architects, Data Protection Officers, and other stakeholders to implement data-by-design processes.
Build expertise and capacity within the pricing team to enable self-service access to data from the data lake in the most efficient way.
Create dynamic reports within Unity Catalog and Power BI.
Eliminate outdated reports and processes and respond swiftly to provide the right data with ease, thereby supporting better pricing decisions and helping us stay ahead as a market leader.


At AXA we work smart, empowering our people to balance their time between home and the office in a way that works best for them, their team and our customers. You'll work at least two days a week (40%) away from home, moving to three days a week (60%) in the future. Away from home means attending the office, visiting clients or attending industry events.

Your Skills & Experience


Proficient in Python with experience using PySpark, DevOps, and Unity Catalog.
Demonstrated skills in extracting, manipulating, and reconciling big data sets.
Ability to work across structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data, extracting insights and identifying connections between disparate data sets.
Solid understanding of information security principles to ensure data is handled and managed in a compliant manner.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to clearly explain complex solutions to diverse audiences.
Understanding of the Commercial Insurance sector would be advantageous.
Familiarity with Acturis would be advantageous.


As a precondition of employment for this role, you must be eligible and authorised to work in the United Kingdom.

How To Apply

To apply, click on the ‘apply for this job’ button, you’ll then need to log in or create a profile to submit your CV. We’re proud to be an Equal Opportunities Employer and don’t discriminate against employees or potential employees based on protected characteristics. If you have a long-term condition or disability and require adjustments during the application or interview process, we’re proud to offer access to the AXA Accessibility Concierge. For our support, please send an email to lauren.cooper@axa-uk.co.uk.

We encourage you to apply for this opportunity as soon as possible, as we may close this advert earlier than the listed closing date.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
This role is being recruited by Scout, our AI recruiter




Scout is hiring on behalf of one of our partner companies and will select the strongest candidates from the ivee network.




Next steps

Step 1. Click apply to create an ivee account

Step 2. Complete your profile and upload your CV

Step 3. That’s it — once your profile is live, Scout can access your CV and start headhunting you for roles that match your skills.




Company description

A fast-growing scale-up using data to drive smarter decisions and unlock growth.




Role overview

The Data Analyst will play a key role in helping the business make better decisions through data.

You’ll analyse datasets, build dashboards, and generate insights that support teams across the organisation. This role involves working closely with product, marketing, and commercial teams to understand performance, identify opportunities, and translate data into actionable recommendations.




You’ll also help improve how the business collects, manages, and uses data, ensuring teams have reliable insights to guide their strategy.




Key responsibilities

Analyse datasets to identify trends, patterns, and growth opportunities
Build dashboards and reports that track key business metrics
Work with stakeholders across teams to answer data-driven questions
Translate complex data into clear insights and recommendations
Write queries and extract data from databases using SQL
Support experimentation and performance analysis for marketing and product initiatives
Ensure data quality and help improve internal data processes
Present findings and insights to internal stakeholders




What we’re looking for

2–4 years of experience in data analysis or analytics roles
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Proficiency with Excel, SQL, and data analysis tools
Experience with data visualisation tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker
Ability to communicate complex insights clearly to non-technical teams
Strong attention to detail and curiosity about business performance
Experience working with large datasets or multiple data sources
Bachelor’s degree in data, statistics, economics, maths, computer science, or a related field (or equivalent experience)




How to apply:




Click apply to create an ivee account.




Upload your CV and write a short bio to start getting headhunted for your next role.

Title: Data Analyst - 6 month FTC
Description:
6 Months Fixed Term Contract

2 days per week Hybrid, initial onboarding - more flexible thereafter.

As a Data Analyst in the Analytics team, you'll play a dedicated role supporting a live strategic transaction workstream, producing investor-grade analytics and data outputs that inform decision-making throughout the process.

In this role, you'll:


Restructure, normalise, and repackage historical and current data sets to investor-grade presentation standards, ensuring outputs are clearly reconciled and defensible under external scrutiny
Respond to ad-hoc data pull and analysis requests from internal leadership, financial advisors, and legal counsel, often under tight turnaround times
Support the preparation of management presentations, data rooms, and information memoranda, translating complex commercial data into clear, credible, stakeholder-ready outputs
Build and maintain analytical models and reconciliation tools to support financial modelling, scenario analysis, and due diligence workstreams
Ensure data consistency, auditability, and version control across all transaction-related outputs, acting as a quality gate for buyer-facing materials
Collaborate with the wider analytics function to cross-reference outputs and maintain alignment with BAU reporting, minimising the risk of inconsistencies across the business


We're looking for someone who:

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Role overview

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Data Analyst to join our IT and Data team. You will work with colleagues across fundraising, marketing, finance and services to understand reporting needs and turn them into trusted datasets, dashboards and insights that help us raise vital funds and demonstrate impact for children and young people affected by cancer. Using Salesforce and Tableau as core tools, you will:




Key Responsibilities

Partner with teams across fundraising, marketing, finance and services to gather requirements and translate them into clear reporting and insight solutions.
Build, maintain and optimise Tableau dashboards that track key charity metrics (e.g., income, supporter engagement, campaign performance and operational KPIs).
Query, extract and analyse data from Salesforce (CRM) to produce accurate reporting for fundraising activity, supporter journeys and internal decision-making.
Document data definitions, business logic and reporting standards so colleagues have a shared understanding of key measures and KPIs.
Identify trends, anomalies and opportunities within datasets and communicate insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders, with sensitivity to our cause and supporters.
Support data quality monitoring and data governance in Salesforce, helping maintain clean, reliable and compliant records in line with GDPR and organisational policies.







Children with Cancer UK

At Children with Cancer UK, our vision is a world where every child and young person survives cancer. Every day, 10 children are diagnosed with cancer in the UK, 2 terminally. Of the 8 who survive, 5 will suffer long-term effects of their treatment. We fund vital research and provide support to families affected by childhood cancer.




We are the leading childhood cancer charity in the UK, supporting families and childhood cancer research since 1988. Over the last 35 years, we've developed kinder treatments, improved public and scientific understanding of childhood cancer and raised over £300 million along with investing in over 200 research projects to help fight the injustice of cancer in children.




Person Specification




Experience

Experience or strong familiarity with Salesforce CRM (or similar), including reporting, views, and understanding data objects (e.g., contacts/supporters, donations, campaigns).
Experience using Tableau (or similar BI tool) to build dashboards and data visualisations for a range of audiences.
Salesforce Administrator, Salesforce Certified Associate (or willingness to work towards certification).
Experience working with charity/fundraising data (e.g., appeals, events, regular giving, major donors) or supporter care reporting.

Skills & Abilities

Solid understanding of data analysis principles, including data quality, validation and the ability to interpret and present findings clearly.
Strong communication skills, able to translate complex data into clear insight for colleagues and stakeholders across the charity.
Comfortable working with structured data using tools such as spreadsheets, data extracts and basic querying, with an understanding of confidentiality and appropriate data handling.
Awareness of GDPR and data protection principles, and/or experience supporting data governance and consent management.
Exposure to ETL/data integration tools and an interest in developing skills in data engineering and automation.

Other attributes

High attention to detail, a problem-solving mindset and a proactive attitude to improving processes and data quality.
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver to deadlines in a collaborative, mission-driven environment.




Please note: If you don’t match all our essential criteria but have other relevant experiences and skills, please do apply. We would love to hear from you.




Other information

At Children with Cancer UK we value diversity, and we are committed to creating an inclusive culture where everyone is able to be themselves and to reach their full potential. The statements are not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and skills required for this position.




How to Apply

Please submit your CV and cover letter.




You must be eligible to work in the UK to apply for this vacancy.

Title: Associate, Data Analytics
Description:
About the Role
FRA’s Forensic Technology practice is a specialist global team at the intersection of data, technology, and high-stakes financial crime and regulatory investigations. We support multinational corporations, regulators, and legal counsel in navigating complex issues including financial crime, regulatory enforcement, and litigation.
As a Data Analytics Associate, you will work on some of the most sensitive and complex matters, contributing to data-driven insights that directly inform regulatory outcomes, legal strategies, and executive decision-making. You will have the opportunity to work with large multifaceted datasets across multiple industries such as Aerospace and Defense, Gambling, Manufacturing, Crypto and Financial Services. Unlike larger firms, you will operate in lean, high-performing teams with direct exposure to senior stakeholders and subject matter experts.

What You’ll Do
You will contribute across the full analytics lifecycle, with increasing ownership as you develop:
Design data workflows spanning data collection, ETL, analysis, visualisation, and delivery across structured and semi-structured datasets
Apply advanced analytical techniques to identify patterns of fraud, corruption, misconduct, and financial crime
Implement analytical workflows that are fully reproducible, deterministic, and auditable, ensuring that outputs can be independently validated and withstand scrutiny in regulatory and legal contexts
Work with complex enterprise data (ERP systems, transactional data, communications, logs) to reconstruct events and generate evidence-based insights
Develop scalable analytical solutions using modern data tools and programming languages
Translate analytical outputs into clear, defensible insights for non-technical stakeholders including legal counsel, regulators, and senior executives
Collaborate closely with investigators, forensic accountants, and legal teams on cross-border matters
Maintain rigorous documentation of data sources, transformations, assumptions, and methodologies to establish clear audit trails and evidentiary integrity
Contribute to the development of reusable tools, methodologies, and intellectual property within the practice
Technology & Data Environment
You will work with a modern and evolving technology stack, including:
SQL-based data platforms (e.g., SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Oracle, DuckDB)
Python for data processing, statistical analysis, and automation
Data visualisation tools (Power BI) for storytelling and reporting
Professional development environments and tooling (e.g., version control systems such as Git, and modern IDEs such as VS Code) to support controlled, traceable, and reproducible analytical workflows
Cloud platforms (primarily Microsoft Azure)
Exposure to machine learning, network analytics, and entity resolution techniques
What We’re Looking For
Strong academic background in a quantitative or analytical field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or similar)
Demonstrated proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (Python preferred)
Experience working with large, complex datasets and deriving actionable insights
Experience developing analytics that are reproducible, traceable, and methodologically robust, particularly in environments subject to external scrutiny
A structured and rigorous approach to problem-solving, with high attention to detail
Ability to communicate complex concepts with clarity to both technical and non-technical audiences
A proactive mindset, with the ability to operate effectively both independently and within small teams
Additional Experience (Advantageous)
Experience in consulting, forensic analytics, or data-driven investigations
Familiarity with ERP systems or transactional datasets (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Dynamics)
Exposure to fraud detection, financial crime analytics, or compliance monitoring
Experience applying statistical and advanced analytical techniques (e.g., hypothesis testing, regression, anomaly detection, or machine learning)
Experience working with version control systems (e.g., Git) and structured development environments (e.g., VS Code or similar), with an understanding of their role in ensuring traceability, collaboration, and reproducibility
Knowledge of data privacy, regulatory frameworks, or eDiscovery processes
Experience with cloud or big data technologies
Professional certifications (e.g., CFE, CAMS, or relevant technology certifications)
Languages
Fluency in English is required
Professional proficiency in French strongly preferred
Additional languages (German, Arabic) are advantageous
About Us
FRA is a market leader in regulatory compliance, financial investigations, and data analytics. At FRA, we are regularly engaged to provide expertise on some of the world’s largest multi-jurisdictional investigations and compliance matters and are consistently recognized as a global market leader. 

www.forensicrisk.com

LinkedIn Page

FRA is an equal opportunity employer committed to hiring the most qualified candidates based on skills, experience, and potential. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

Title: Commerce Data Analyst
Description:
Condé Nast is a global media company producing the highest quality content with a footprint of more than 1 billion consumers in 32 territories through print, digital, video and social platforms. The company’s portfolio includes many of the world’s most respected and influential media properties including Vogue, Vanity Fair, Glamour, Self, GQ, The New Yorker, Condé Nast Traveler/Traveller, Allure, AD, Bon Appétit and Wired, among others.

Job Description

Location:

London, GB

Condé Nast is a global media company, home to iconic brands including Vogue, GQ, Glamour, CN Traveller, Vanity Fair, Wired, The World of Interiors, House & Garden and Tatler, among many others.

We are headquartered in New York and London and operate in 32 markets worldwide, with a footprint of more than 1 billion consumers across print, digital, video and social platforms.

Condé Nast thrives on collaboration, and our teams come together in the office four days a week (Monday - Thursday).

We value diversity of background, views and cultures. We celebrate people for their personal qualities, skills and contributions, recognising the power our brands have to influence and shape culture.

The Role

To be successful in this role, you must be highly strategic with exceptional ability to translate data into compelling narratives and actionable recommendations. You're equally comfortable building frameworks for recurring analysis as you are diving into ad hoc investigations that uncover hidden opportunities. You have strong technical capabilities—able to troubleshoot data pipeline issues, write SQL queries, and coordinate with engineering teams to resolve systemic challenges. You thrive in fast-paced environments, managing multiple stakeholders across markets and time zones while maintaining rigorous standards for data accuracy and insight quality.

What will you be doing?

Strategic Analysis & Business Support


Support regional leads on monthly brand performance reviews across Western Europe markets, empowering teams to understand their data and developing clear recommendations to move performance forward
Conduct ad hoc deep-dive analyses using data outside standard dashboards to identify growth opportunities, optimization levers, and emerging trends
Analyze monetization opportunities to grow EPC through category classification, commission structures, network performance, GMV, AOV, and other key levers
Partner with finance on revenue reporting, interpreting commerce trends and providing context to support financial planning


Data Infrastructure & Technical Operations


Build and maintain intuitive self-service dashboards and data visualizations; set up new reporting systems to help teams access and understand their data
Identify and diagnose data discrepancies or pipeline issues; work directly with engineering teams to implement fixes
Use SQL to conduct custom analysis and troubleshoot technical challenges; ensure data quality across all reporting systems


Process Development & Cross-Functional Coordination


Create repeatable frameworks and methodologies for strategic analysis; standardize performance review processes across markets
Liaise across stakeholders in multiple markets, synthesizing inputs and translating complex technical concepts for non-technical audiences
Establish clear protocols for cross-functional collaboration and coordinate effectively across different time zones


Who you are:


Highly strategic thinker with exceptional data storytelling abilities—you don't just present numbers, you build narratives that drive decision-making
Strong technical foundation with proficiency in SQL (required); experience with Python, Tableau, Business Objects, or similar analytical tools is a plus
Advanced in Excel/Google Sheets with ability to build sophisticated models and analyses
Demonstrated ability to troubleshoot technical issues and coordinate with engineering and teams to implement solutions
Understanding of GDPR and other local laws regarding data usage
Experience developing repeatable processes and frameworks for strategic analysis
Proven track record of managing multiple stakeholders across different markets and organizational levels
Strong problem-solving skills with ability to work independently on complex, ambiguous challenges
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, able to distill complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations
Background in eCommerce operations; affiliate marketing experience (preferably publisher-side) is highly valued
Deep understanding of conversion funnel dynamics, margin management, and affiliate KPIs
Degree in Maths, Computer Science, Economics, or similar quantitative field preferred


Does this sound like you?

Please upload your CV and cover letter/portfolio, which highlights why you'd love to take on this role and why you're a great match for what we're looking for.

We value the time and effort behind every application. All submissions are reviewed by a member of our talent team - we don’t use AI-assisted technology to review applications.

What benefits do we offer?


25 days holiday (plus bank holidays) and extra days of annual leave if you move house or want to volunteer.
You’ll have access to a competitive pension scheme, Bupa Private Healthcare, Season ticket loans and eye tests.
We offer a range of tools to support your wellbeing, including core hours, 10 remote days (from home or a country with a Condé Nast office location), access to our Employee Assistance Programme, corporate gym membership and cycle to work scheme.
We’re a dog friendly office, plus you’ll enjoy discounts and magazine subscriptions, keeping you up to date with all things Condé Nast.
We encourage personal and professional growth through the Condé Nast Learning Hub where you’ll find an extensive portfolio of learning courses and training, available in local languages.
Our Employee Resource Groups provide a platform for employees to identify shared objectives, exchange ideas, and work on community priorities for our global workforce.


What happens next?

If you are interested in this opportunity, please apply below, and we will review your application as soon as possible. You can update your resume or upload a cover letter at any time by accessing your candidate profile.

Condé Nast is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, age, familial status and other legally protected characteristics.

Title: Data Analyst - Food Performance
Description:
We’re looking for a Food Performance & Nutrition analyst to undertake advanced analytics in food performance, at the intersection of product, pet satisfaction, and customer behavior. You’ll be instrumental in building and maintaining data pipelines, defining metrics, and applying statistical rigor to uncover insights that drive better food experiences for both pets and customers.

You’ll partner with Product, Food teams, our Tailoring Technology workstream, and Infrastructure & Analytics Engineering to formalise how we evaluate food performance, integrate new nutrition and quality control datasets, and support scalable experimentation and optimisation across the food domain.

Changing the world of pet food for good

We’re Tails.com, a dog food subscription company with a big difference. We create truly tailored food for each and every dog we serve. We start by asking people a few simple questions about their dog. Then we use that information to create their dog’s unique Tails.com recipe – so their dog gets exactly the nutrition they need, in the taste they love, delivered to their door every month. It’s clever stuff.

We’ve got bold plans. Having created an entirely new category in pet food, we’re now scaling fast in the UK and Europe – backed by Purina, one of the world’s largest pet food companies. You’ll join a bunch of smart people working towards the same goals – and a bunch of smart-ish dogs, all working on their own thing.

Together, we’ll change the world of pet food for good.

The data team at Tails.com sits within the Tech Function at Tails.com, and partners across the whole business. Our team covers the entire spectrum of data-related tasks, from collecting and processing data to synthesising impactful, actionable insights and conducting statistically rigorous experiments. We play a vital role in helping individuals at Tails.com make informed decisions, driving the continued success and growth of our business.

Build our brand

We’re building a brand, fast. We set ambitious goals. We challenge and support each other in equal measure. At the pace we’re moving, we prefer to test ideas and learn quickly rather than spend months building a business case. That means we celebrate when things go right, and we learn when things go wrong.

Sound like a team you want to join? Here’s how you’ll make a difference:

The role

We’re looking for a Food Performance & Nutrition Data Analyst to lead advanced analytics at the intersection of product, pet satisfaction, and customer behaviour. You’ll be instrumental in building new data pipelines, defining metrics, and applying statistical rigor to uncover insights that drive better food experiences for both pets and customers.

Your Responsibilities


Design and own a food performance framework, incorporating both customer-reported and behaviourally observed signals which controls for multiple confounding variables
Lead the setup of performance monitoring and reporting for food acceptance and product-related behaviour signals (e.g. cancellations, flavour switch rates, repeat rates).
Support product and range teams in the testing and launch of new products, leading on needs assessment, feasibility analysis, nutritional impact evaluation, and defining required changes through to launch and successful adoption.
Build upon our existing simulation and measurement tooling for our Tailoring Technology workstream, including frameworks to evaluate efficacy of our recommendations and optimisation for the best customer outcomes once launched.
Conduct analysis on the impacts of evolving our product recommendations — identifying behavioural drivers of cancellation, satisfaction shifts, and long-term retention effects.
Collaborate on SKU remapping, product catalogue development, and nutrition data integrations, ensuring data readiness and analytic coverage across initiatives by building and updating pipelines and rigorously testing data validity
Ingest and leverage high-dimensional nutrition and NIR machine data from our QC processes to evaluate manufacturing tolerances, batch variability, and their related impact on customer satisfaction
Deliver clear, actionable and impactful insights and documentation for leadership and cross-functional stakeholders with varied levels of technical knowledge


The skills you’ll bring:


2–4+ years of experience in data analytics, or applied product analytics
Strong communication skills – able to translate technical insight into clear business recommendations
Strong proficiency in SQL (Python desirable as well), and experience working with complex, messy, real-world data from multiple sources
Hands-on experience with metric design and behavior-based performance measurement
Highly competent time management, with the ability to source, generate, prioritise and deliver on the most impactful work collaboratively with key partners
Skilled in data visualisation and storytelling using BI tools (e.g. ThoughtSpot, Looker, Tableau)
Experience working with and influencing cross-functionally (Product, Proposition, Engineering, R&D, and Data teams)


Even better if you have:


Familiarity with pet nutrition, foodtech, or manufacturing data (e.g. NIR, QC workflows)
Demonstrated ability to ingest and model high-dimensional manufacturing or QC datasets
Experience designing and analysing experiments and/or simulations for product feature impact
Exposure to using survey data and leveraging unstructured customer feedback for analytics.
Understanding of DTC or subscription business models and customer lifecycle analytics


What's in it for you?


Competitive salary, reviewed annually
Flexible core hours, giving you true work life balance
Hybrid role to ensure this role works for you (2 days in London office)
25 days of holiday (excluding bank holidays) which increases over time to a max of 27 days
9/10 day working fortnight to enhance your wellbeing without a reduction in pay and leave allowance - this totals over 20 additional days off in 2025!
Optional 5 days unpaid leave and 1 paid volunteer day each year
When you need a change of scenery, you can work from abroad 2 weeks every 6 months
Health insurance for you, paid by Tails.com
Extended maternity, paternity, shared parental and adoption pay. 6 months at 70% pay
Flexible paid care leave to support immediate dependants, people close to you and pets
In house L&D team, with access to year round courses to help drive your development
50% discount on all Tails.com
Discounted gym membership


What’s next?

If this sounds like it matches your experience and what you’d love to do, we can’t wait to hear from you! If you’re unsure whether you fit our criteria exactly, please get in touch anyway. And because we believe that diverse teams perform better, we’d especially love to hear from you if you’re from an under-represented demographic.

Here’s a taster of how our recruitment process works:


Once you have applied your CV will be reviewed by the Talent Acquisition Team.
If selected, you will then have an Initial screening call with the Talent Acquisition Team.
If selected, you will then have an interview with the Hiring Manager to understand more about your skills and experience (60 mins).
Should you be successful, the next stage will be a take-home assignment followed by a video call with the hiring manager and Head of to present your findings and field questions about your approach and the overall project (75 minutes)
If successful, you will be invited to a final interview (60 mins).


Our Commitment

We’re proud to be an active equal opportunity employer. We want to give everyone a fair chance to join us in changing the world of pet food for good. We do this by conducting a structured recruitment process for all candidates, as well as actively promoting our roles to communities that are under-represented at Tails.com.

During your interview process you’ll meet some of our team with varying levels of responsibility and experience. We want to give every candidate a fair interview process and if you need any reasonable adjustments made, please let us know.

Title: Associate Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Hansen (ASX: HSN) is a global provider of software and services to the energy, water and communications industries. With our award-winning software suite, we help customers in over 80 countries to create and deliver new products and services, engage with customers, and control and manage critical revenue management and customer support processes.

Traditionally Hansen has been known for our billing and customer care solutions for utilities and energy companies; and for our BSS/OSS solutions for the communications industry. Yet today our solutions are far broader than this – from enabling the strategic trading of energy resources, to gaining real insights from the data tsunami that smart meters enable and the ability to quickly sell products through our Catalog -driven solutions. Put simply, our solutions are essential ingredients in our customers’ commercial business model, providing them the ability to create and deliver these essential services, charge for them, and establish and maintain lasting relationships with their end customers.

About The Role

We’re looking for a Associate Data Analyst to help build a modern, AI-enabled people analytics capability. In this role, you’ll analyse workforce, productivity, and utilisation data to uncover insights that drive better decision-making across the organisation.

This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate or early‑career analyst with strong analytical foundations and a passion for applying data science and AI to real-world people and workforce challenges.

What You’ll Do


Analyse people, workforce, and productivity data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities
Design and deliver dashboards, reports, and visualisations for leaders using tools such as Power BI
Leverage AI tools (including Copilot and large language models) to accelerate analysis, insight generation, and reporting
Translate complex data into clear, practical insights and recommendations for non-technical stakeholders
Support the continuous improvement of people metrics, reporting standards, and analytical approaches
Partner with HR, Finance, and IT to ensure data quality, consistency, and responsible use of people data


What You’ll Bring


A degree (or near completion) in Data Science, Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, Psychology, or a related field
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
Hands-on experience with data analysis tools such as Python, SQL, R, or similar
Familiarity with data visualisation tools (e.g. Power BI, Tableau)
Exposure to AI tools, machine learning concepts, or advanced analytics
Ability to clearly communicate insights to both technical and non-technical audiences
Curiosity, a strong learning mindset, and a genuine interest in people analytics and AI


Why Join Us


Help shape a next-generation, AI-first people analytics function
Apply data and AI to meaningful, real-world organisational decisions
Gain exposure to senior stakeholders in a global environment
Benefit from strong learning and development opportunities across analytics, AI, and enterprise productivity


We are proud to be an equal opportunities employer. Hansen prides itself on celebrating diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees, even extending this to how we work with our customers, partners and suppliers. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. If you require any adjustments or accommodations during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Accepting Applications Until

8 May 2026

Job Description

Your new role: Data Analyst

We’re looking for a mid-level Data Analyst with 3–5+ years of commercial experience in a data analytics role, ideally within a fast-paced, data-driven business.

As a Data Analyst at Global, you’ll combine your curiosity for insight and your enthusiasm for delivering effective analytical solutions to help evolve Global’s market-leading business.

Key Responsibilities


Building analytical solutions to meet complex business needs (40%): You will develop a deep understanding of the business, then use that knowledge to effectively gather requirements for pieces of work and create innovative solutions that meet those requirements. Insightful data visualisations in Tableau will be your bread and butter in the reports and deliverables you create.
Solving problems to improve robustness in data processes (30%): You will adapt to the changing data landscape, identifying the root causes of issues that arise and finding efficient solutions to them. Your problem solving skills will support your fellow teams in data through collaboration and clear communication.
Developing your skills and supporting the growth of the team (30%): You will apply a continuous learning approach to your work, improving your methods and expanding your perspectives to realise your potential. You will support your team members, uplifting your team to be greater than the sum of its parts.


What You’ll Love About This Role


Think Big: Help us to define the future of analytics at Global, bringing innovation and big ideas to drive the department forwards
Own It: Take responsibility for your successes and failures, being proud of your work and the effort that went into it
Keep it Simple: Creative effective solutions to business problems without overcomplicating the task at hand
Better Together: Collaborate with a team of like-minded data individuals to produce excellent pieces of work in a supportive and friendly environment


What Success Looks Like

In your first few months, you’ll have:


Gained a strong understanding of our business operating model within our DAX business, and some understanding of our classic audio, outdoor and consumer businesses
Built up a foundation of knowledge of the main data warehouses, systems, and data workflows that your squad works with
Gotten hands on with the data, supporting different projects through building solutions, testing data outputs or answering business questions
Spent time with team members and business stakeholders to understand the ongoing projects and workstreams in DAX, and added your ideas into the mix to help shape the direction that the team moves in


What You’ll Need


3–5+ years of commercial experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytical role
Demonstrated experience owning analytical projects from requirement gathering through to delivery and stakeholder presentation
Experience working with large, complex datasets in a commercial environment
Analytical Mindset: A brain with more questions than answers that you use to challenge the assumptions made by both yourself and others. A logical process for dissecting systems to find the actionable insight within.
Attention to Detail: A rigorous approach to problem solving and a keen eye for detail, to help you diagnose the cause behind the symptoms you see and prescribe the right solution for the situation.
Visualisation Expertise: Experience producing high quality reporting with BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI, and experience querying and wrangling data with SQL.
Collaboration, Coaching and Support: Drive for creating and maintaining an inclusive environment where diverse views and experiences are welcomed and celebrated. Energy to encourage and help those around you so the team grows together.
Communication: The ability to break down complex concepts into their core components to explain them to others, including explaining your fantastic ideas to others to bring them on board!

Title: BI Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title:

BI Data Analyst

Contract Type:

Permanent

Time Type:

Full time

Job Description:

Scope of the role

Reporting to the Global IT Project Manager, the BI Data Analyst is responsible for designing, delivering, and supporting enterprise-grade business intelligence and analytics solutions using Microsoft Power BI and the Power Platform, with a strong focus on supporting commodity trading (e.g. oil, metal, gas, power, LNG, derivatives), shipping and trade lifecycle activities (e.g. operation, risk management, contract, settlement, letter of credit, etc.) . The role is critical for translating trading-specific data insights into actionable business decisions.

The role acts as a subject-matter expert (SME) for Power BI while also possessing strong business analysis capabilities, enabling data-driven decision-making through high-quality dashboards, governed datasets, automation, and strong stakeholder engagement.

Main Responsibilities


Strategic Collaboration & Business Alignment
Partner with business users, senior management, and IT stakeholders to understand analytical needs and translate them into actionable BI solutions.
Work closely with commodity traders, risk managers, and business teams to ensure that the analytical solutions are aligned with market, trading and operation activities.
Gather, document, and validate business requirements; recommend analytical approaches aligned with business objectives.
Translate complex commodity trading business problems into technical designs that deliver clear insights and measurable business value.
Proactively engage with senior business stakeholders to identify areas of improvement in trading and shipping operations and support business development through data-driven insights.
Innovation & Product Development
Design, develop, and maintain Power BI datasets, reports, and dashboards to meet evolving business requirements.
Build and deliver end-to-end analytical and automation solutions using Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, and AI Builder tailored to the commodity trading and shipping business.
Build solution prototypes where needed to validate concepts and accelerate delivery.
Work closely with the data architect and data engineering team to design and deliver analytics-ready datasets through data ingestion, cleansing, and transformation.
Document analytical logic, data models, and datasets to support advanced analytics and future enhancements
BI Adoption & Enablement
Act as the go-to expert for Power BI and data analytics–related user queries within the commodity trading and shipping domain.
Conduct Power BI end-user training sessions and provide ongoing guidance and best-practice support.
Promote effective BI usage patterns and self-service analytics across the organization
Advocate for the importance of data governance and business rules specific to commodity trading and shipping to enhance the accuracy and timeliness of trading decisions.
Operational Excellence
Enforce BI governance standards and best practices to maintain data quality, consistency, and auditability, ensuring that data used in trading decisions is highly reliable and actionable.
Monitor and optimize report performance and dataset refreshes to ensure stability and scalability.
Maintain clear technical documentation, release artifacts, and operational procedures



Profile


Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Business Analytics, or a related field.
Microsoft certifications in Power BI / Power Platform / Fabric are highly preferred.
Strong understanding of commodity trading environments is required (oil, metal, gas, power, LNG, derivatives). In-depth knowledge of the trading lifecycle, including market trends, operation, PnL, positions, settlements and risk management, is essential.
Experience working with trading data (e.g., PnL, risk, positions, settlements) and ETRM systems is highly preferred.
Strong expertise in Microsoft Power Platform, with Power BI as a core specialization (SME level).
Excellent SQL skills and solid understanding of data modelling concepts.
Strong business analysis skills, capable of interpreting trading requirements and translating them into technical deliverables.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Strong stakeholder management skills, capable of exceeding stakeholder expectations, particularly in the context of trading and business analysis.
Self-motivated, proactive, and well-organized, with strong time-management skills.
Comfortable working under pressure in a fast-paced, trading-focused environment.
Team player with a collaborative mindset.
Fluent in English (spoken and written). Any other language is an asset.



If you think the open position you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply!

Our people make all the difference in our success.

  • Monitor and optimize report performance and dataset refreshes to ensure stability and scalability.
  • Maintain clear technical documentation, release artifacts, and operational procedures


Profile

  • Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Business Analytics, or a related field.
  • Microsoft certifications in Power BI / Power Platform / Fabric are highly preferred.
  • Strong understanding of commodity trading environments is required (oil, metal, gas, power, LNG, derivatives). In-depth knowledge of the trading lifecycle, including market trends, operation, PnL, positions, settlements and risk management, is essential.
  • Experience working with trading data (e.g., PnL, risk, positions, settlements) and ETRM systems is highly preferred.
  • Strong expertise in Microsoft Power Platform, with Power BI as a core specialization (SME level).
  • Excellent SQL skills and solid understanding of data modelling concepts.
  • Strong business analysis skills, capable of interpreting trading requirements and translating them into technical deliverables.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
  • Strong stakeholder management skills, capable of exceeding stakeholder expectations, particularly in the context of trading and business analysis.
  • Self-motivated, proactive, and well-organized, with strong time-management skills.
  • Comfortable working under pressure in a fast-paced, trading-focused environment.
  • Team player with a collaborative mindset.
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written). Any other language is an asset.


If you think the open position you see is right for you, we encourage you to apply!

Our people make all the difference in our success.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Air Apps

At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.

Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.

Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.

The Role

As a Data Analyst at Air Apps, you will be responsible for analyzing and interpreting data to drive business insights and optimize product performance. You will create reports, dashboards, and performance metrics to help teams make informed decisions and improve user experience.

This role requires a data-driven mindset, strong analytical skills, and experience with visualization tools to turn complex data into actionable insights.


This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


Responsibilities


Analyze user behavior, product performance, and key business metrics to identify trends and opportunities.
Create and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or Google Data Studio.
Collect, clean, and structure large datasets to ensure accuracy and usability.
Define and track KPIs for marketing, product, and business performance.
Work with SQL, Python, or R to extract, analyze, and manipulate data.
Collaborate with product managers, engineers, and marketing teams to provide data-driven insights.
Conduct A/B testing and experiment analysis to improve product features and user engagement.
Identify bottlenecks and opportunities in user journeys and customer funnels.
Present findings in a clear and compelling way to stakeholders and leadership teams.
Stay up to date with industry trends, analytics best practices, and emerging data tools.


Requirements


Around 3+ years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related field.
Proficiency in SQL for data querying and manipulation.
Experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, Google Data Studio).
Strong analytical skills with experience in Python or R for data analysis.
Knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and statistical analysis.
Experience with Google Analytics, Mixpanel, Amplitude, or similar user analytics tools.
Strong problem-solving skills and ability to communicate insights to non-technical stakeholders.
Experience working with large datasets and cloud-based data warehouses (BigQuery, Redshift, Snowflake).
Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipeline optimization is a plus.
Ability to work in a fast-paced, data-driven environment and manage multiple projects.


What benefits are we offering?


Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
Annual Bonus
Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
Childcare support.
Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
Meals 100% free at the hub.


Diversity & Inclusion

At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.

Application Disclaimer

At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.

Compensation Range: €50K - €63K

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
London

Analytics – Product Analytics /

Employee - Permanent /

Hybrid

apply for this job

Our Story

Hello there. We’re Zopa.

We started our journey back in 2005, building the first ever peer-to-peer lending company. Fast forward to 2020 and we launched Zopa Bank. A bank that listens to what our customers don’t like about finance and does the opposite. We’re redefining what it feels like to work in finance. Our vision for a new era of banking puts people front and centre — we’ve built a business that empowers everyone to aim high, every day, to move finance forward. Find out more about our fantastic offerings at Zopa.com!

We’re incredibly proud of our achievements and none of it would be possible without the amazing team here. It’s not just industry awards we’re winning, we’ve also been named in the top three UK’s Most Loved Workplaces.

If you embrace unconventional challenges, are unafraid to think differently and are driven to make an outsized impact, you’ll thrive here at Zopa, so join us, and make it count. Want to see us in action? Follow us on Instagram @zopalife

At Zopa, analysts are the people who inspire ideas and strategies to drive our business forward and make our products better for customers.

Our analysts are a mix of creative problem solvers and number whizzes with a passion for translating numbers into business opportunities and delivering insights to support decision-making.

They are also strong communicators, able to present data clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Our analysts are embedded in the business and work closely with teams across Product, Engineering and the wider organisation.

We are currently looking for Product Analysts (level 10) to join our UPL, Payments, CRM and Mobile App product tribes. Your role is to be the key person generating the insight that helps us to make data-driven decisions to improve our product.

Tribe Focus Areas

While the core role is consistent, each tribe offers a different problem space and stage of maturity:


Unsecured Personal Loans (UPL) Tribe (Established / Optimisation & Growth) Focused on accelerating growth in a well-established, high-performing product. The role centres on optimising the lending funnel, improving conversion and balancing trade-offs across risk and commercial performance.


Best suited to someone commercially minded who enjoys refining mature products, driving incremental gains and working on high-visibility, revenue-critical problems.


CRM (Evolving / High Impact) We’re investing in CRM analytics to help us move from campaign-led execution to a more customer-led strategy. With over 1.7 million customers, this role will play a key part in shaping how we target audiences, measure performance, and understand the true value of our communications.


You’ll help bring consistency, working across CRM, Product and Marketing to deliver insight that drives better customer outcomes. It’s an opportunity to join at an early stage and have real influence on how this capability evolves.

This role will suit someone who enjoys turning data into practical insight, is curious about customer behaviour, and is comfortable working in a fast-paced environment where they can drive real customer impact.


Mobile App Tribe (Platform Ownership / Growth & Commercial Impact) The mobile app is how customers experience Zopa, from onboarding to managing their money and discovering new products. This role owns the app as a horizontal platform, while also being accountable for key commercial outcomes such as in-app conversion and product adoption.


You’ll define and track metrics, identify opportunities to improve performance, and drive changes that deliver both customer value and measurable business impact.

This role will suit someone who enjoys owning metrics end-to-end, balancing customer experience with commercial performance, and working across teams to drive meaningful outcomes.

A day in the life...


Support Product, Engineering and other stakeholders with analysis to inform product decisions
Work with existing metrics and support improvements to how we measure product performance
Analyse datasets to identify trends and highlight areas for further investigation
Break down problems into clear analytical approaches, with guidance where needed
Support the design and analysis of experiments to test product changes
Build dashboards and reports to track performance and enable decision-making
Deliver deep-dives and ad hoc analysis to answer business questions
Communicate insights clearly to a range of stakeholders
Contribute to analytical projects, taking ownership of defined pieces of work


About You...


Strong SQL skills and experience working with data
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker or Power BI; Python is a bonus
Experience in a data-led environment such as fintech, banking, consulting or a high-growth start-up
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
Comfortable turning data into clear, actionable insight
Able to communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Willing to ask questions, seek feedback and continuously improve
Able to work effectively in cross-functional teams
Please note - no candidate will meet every single desired qualification. If your experience looks a little different from what we’ve identified and you think you can bring value to the role, we’d love to learn more about you!*


At Zopa we value flexible ways of working.

We value face-to-face collaboration and a good work-life balance. This hybrid role requires you to come to our London office 2-3 days a week.

You'll also have the option of working from abroad for up to 120 days a year!* But no matter where you are, we’ll make sure you’ve got everything you need to thrive, both in your work and home life, from day one.


Subject to having the right to work in the country of choice


Diversity Statement

Zopa is proud to offer a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and backgrounds leads to better products for our customers and a unique company culture for our people. We are made up of nearly 50 nationalities, have a DE&I forum made up of Zopians wanting to make a difference and we are proud of our culture where everyone can bring their full self to work. Our approach to DE&I is reflected in our hiring process so please let us know if you require any reasonable adjustments.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
An innovative organisation in the public sector space is looking for a Performance and Data Analyst. This role is central to ensuring the accuracy of statutory returns and providing high-quality performance analysis to support strategic decision-making for children and young people with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND).

What You'll Be Doing


Lead and deliver statutory SEND data returns including SEN2 to high standards.
Develop and maintain data systems and reporting tools to support service objectives.
Produce high-quality analysis, dashboards, and performance reports for senior leaders and schools.
Collaborate with educational institutions to ensure accurate data submissions and resolve validation errors.
Translate legislative and operational requirements into technical data specifications for system development.


What We're Looking For


Extensive experience extracting, manipulating, and reporting data from specialist systems.
Strong technical proficiency in SQL or specific experience with Education and SEND data.
Ability to communicate complex information clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Experience developing analytical tools and dashboards, ideally using Power BI.
Legally authorized to work in the United Kingdom.


What's On Offer


Competitive hourly rate with PAYE and Umbrella options.
Initial contract with strong potential for extension.
Hybrid working arrangements to support work-life balance.
Opportunity to directly influence services for children and young people.


Apply via Hyra to be considered for this opportunity.

Title: Quantitative Data Analyst
Description:
The Wow

Quantitative Analyst - Revolutionizing Sports Betting

Join our team and shape the future of sports betting! We're seeking a highly motivated and innovative Quantitative Analyst to develop and maintain cutting-edge statistical models. You'll play a crucial role in enhancing our market offerings and driving our success in the dynamic world of sports betting. This is an opportunity to leverage your expertise in data science, machine learning, and AI to build models that predict outcomes, manage risk, and unlock new possibilities in micro-markets and derivatives.

 

A taster of what you will be involved with  

As a Quantitative Analyst, you will be a key contributor to our quantitative modelling strategy across multiple areas, including

Core Sports Betting Models Develop and refine models for predicting match outcomes, incorporating advanced statistical methods, machine learning algorithms, and AI techniques.
Player Statistics Models Build and maintain sophisticated models that analyze player performance and predict individual contributions, opening up exciting new betting markets.
Risk Management Models Design and implement robust risk models to optimize our exposure and ensure the long-term sustainability of our operations.
Market Expansion Contribute to the development of innovative market offerings, including derivatives and micro-markets, by creating the necessary underlying models.
Technology Modernization Collaborate on the modernization of our modeling technologies, exploring and implementing new tools and platforms to enhance efficiency and scalability.
Data-Driven Innovation Contribute to the development of new data-driven methodologies, pushing the boundaries of what's possible in sports betting analytics.
Data Collaboration Work closely with the data engineering team to optimize data storage and retrieval processes, ensuring seamless access to the information needed for model development.
Data Acquisition Identify and collect relevant data sources to fuel model development and improvement.
Knowledge Sharing Document model methodologies clearly and concisely, effectively communicating complex technical concepts to both technical and non-technical stakeholders across the business.

What we are looking for

You are a highly analytical and results-oriented individual with a passion for sports and a deep understanding of quantitative modelling. You thrive in a fast-paced environment and are eager to contribute to a team that is pushing the boundaries of sports betting analytics.

Programming Skills Proficiency in C# and Python is essential.
Modelling Expertise Strong knowledge of statistical and machine learning modelling techniques.
Communication Skills Excellent communication skills, with the ability to clearly explain complex technical information to diverse audiences.
Industry Knowledge A solid understanding of the sports betting industry, sports trading, and sports modelling.
Problem-Solving Demonstrated ability to solve complex problems and provide clear justifications for chosen methodologies.
Modelling Experience Proven experience in building and deploying mathematical models.
Data Skills Proficient in data retrieval and statistical analysis.
Experience 5+ years of experience in a quantitative role.

Bonus Points

Experience working in the sports betting industry.
Mentoring and team leadership skills.
Experience developing quantitative models using machine learning and AI.
Strong knowledge and skills in data engineering (SQL).
What we offer
A chance to be part of a dynamic and innovative team at the forefront of the sports betting industry.
The opportunity to work on challenging and impactful projects.
A competitive salary and benefits package.
A collaborative and supportive work environment.

If you're ready to take your quantitative skills to the next level and revolutionize the world of sports betting, we encourage you to apply!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Hybrid - London - 4 days per week in the office.

£45,000 to £50,000 +bonus.




The Company

A data-led marketing and technology business that helps organisations understand their customers and deliver more relevant, personalised experiences.




The Role

Work as part of an analytics team to understand client objectives and translate them into clear analytical approaches.
Analyse campaign performance, audience behaviour, and customer interactions to identify what worked, what did not, and what to do next.
Support clients with broader analytical projects such as customer segmentation, identifying high-value customers, and analysing large transactional datasets.
Collaborate with colleagues to build insights and contribute to client presentations and recommendations.
Use data storytelling to turn complex analysis into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders.




Your Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience as a data or insight analyst
Solid SQL capability for querying and analysing large datasets.
Experience using a BI or data visualisation tool, with Tableau preferred but not essential.
Exposure to digital, marketing, advertising, or CRM data is beneficial.
Comfortable working with clients or stakeholders and explaining analytical findings clearly.
Curious, engaged, and motivated to understand the “why” behind the data.




What They Offer

Salary of £45,000 to £50,000 plus a 10% bonus.
The chance to work with well-known consumer brands on high-impact projects.
Clear development opportunities within analytics and client-facing work.
A collaborative team environment with experienced leaders who remain hands-on.




How to Apply

If you are a Data Analyst looking to grow your impact in customer and marketing analytics, apply now to learn more.

Employment Type: Full-time / Permanent




About the Role

We are hiring a Business & Data Analyst with strong London Market insurance expertise to support major transformation initiatives across underwriting, claims, pricing, delegated authority, reinsurance, and regulatory reporting.




This is a fantastic opportunity to work at the intersection of business, data, and technology, helping modernize platforms, improve reporting, and drive data-led decision-making in a highly regulated environment.

Experience in data migration, platform modernization, regulatory reporting

Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Experience working in Agile / Hybrid delivery environments




Why Join Us?

Work on strategic transformation programmes
Hybrid working model
Collaborate with senior stakeholders and industry experts
Opportunity to make a real impact in the London Insurance Market

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Blue Legal are partnering with a renowned global law firm, recognised for its innovative approach and exceptional service. As a Data Analyst in the Business Development team, you’ll be responsible for managing and analysing data, process design and mapping, automating reports and providing insights that drive strategic decision-making. This role is crucial in supporting a key project within the BD team, helping to design and streamline their processes around their use of data.




This role is suitable for candidates with experience working for professional services firms, or large corporate organisations. Please only apply if you can start within a few weeks’ notice and are happy with the contract term.




The Responsibilities:




The candidate will be responsible for gathering, organising, and managing data from multiple sources to ensure its accuracy and reliability for analysis.
They will collaborate closely with the operations and planning team to understand the requirements for data analytics and process mapping.
The role involves analysing data to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for quick wins that can drive action within the function.
The candidate will work with internal teams to identify data sources, define the steps for reporting, and design scalable solutions for automating reports.
They will collaborate with Data Visualisation Designers and Analysts to ensure that the data and reporting requirements are clearly defined for development.
Part of the role includes sharing the outputs of their analysis with relevant stakeholders, incorporating feedback, and refining the analysis as needed.
The individual will stay updated with the latest trends, tools, and methodologies in data analytics, continually seeking to improve the quality and impact of their work.
Maintaining data integrity and adhering to data governance standards is essential, as is ensuring that all processes follow best practices for accuracy and reliability.




The Candidate:




They should have a strong background in data and reporting workflow process mapping, including the ability to perform detailed data forensics when needed.
The candidate must be comfortable working with both structured and unstructured data and have the ability to handle complex datasets effectively.
The individual should have demonstrated experience in collaborating with cross-functional teams to define data needs, reporting processes, and automation steps for scalable reporting.
Familiarity with data visualisation principles is important, and experience working alongside dashboard developers or data designers is a plus.
A strong proficiency in data analysis tools and techniques, such as Excel, SQL, Python, R, Power BI, or Tableau, is essential.




Please note: Due to the specific sectors we work in, only candidates with valid work experience in a Law Firm, Accountancy Firm, Management Consultancy, Property/Construction Firm, Financial Services Firm or a high profile relevant Association or Agency will be considered. We regret that our clients will not accept applications outside of these areas.




Blue Legal offers the services of an employment agency for permanent work and an employment business for temporary work.




For more positions like these, check out our website at www.bluelegal.com or follow us on Twitter for the widest range of roles across the entire professional services sector @bluelegalrec

Title: DATA ANALYST, MARKETING
Description:
About OakNorth

At OakNorth, we’re on a mission to empower the UK’s most ambitious businesses. Since 2015, we’ve lent over $21 billion across the UK and US, helped create more than 58,000 new homes and 36,000 new jobs, and supported hundreds of thousands of personal savers, all while fuelling the UK economy.

As OakNorth continues to grow across multiple products and customer segments, clear, effective communication and rigorous measurement are critical. Marketing plays a central role in how we explain value, drive adoption and support commercial performance across the bank.

The role

We’re looking for a Marketing Data Analyst to join the Marketing team and enable growth across OakNorth’s Lending, Business Banking and Personal Savings products through actionable insights, data-driven strategies and robust measurement frameworks.

This is a hands-on analytics role, central to how marketing and commercial performance is understood and optimised. You will work closely with Marketing, Product, Commercial and Finance teams to deliver commercial intelligence, performance analysis and platform optimisation that improve unit economics, sharpen go-to-market activity and accelerate growth.

You will own key elements of marketing data and analytics, from attribution and reporting to experimentation and insight generation, helping teams understand what is working, where to invest and how to scale effectively.

What You'll Do


Marketing analytics and data management
Own marketing analytics and insight generation, partnering with MarkOps on data quality and enrichment, and performing targeted cleansing as needed to enable accurate analysis and visualisation.
Define and influence the build and maintenance of attribution logic and performance benchmarks to support confident decision making.
Analyse channel efficiency, conversion metrics and customer behaviour to improve marketing efficiency and unit economics.
Design, execute and analyse experiments and A/B tests using sound statistical principles.
Leverage automation and AI-driven approaches to improve insight generation, productivity and scalable marketing execution.
Platforms, tools and systems
Work hands on to analyse, model and visualise marketing performance data.
Build and maintain dashboards and self-serve reporting using visualisation tools such as Looker, Tableau or similar.
Use SQL extensively to query, transform and analyse large, complex datasets.
Work confidently with Excel for analysis, modelling and validation alongside SQL-based workflows.
Support Marketing Operations team with CRM platforms, particularly Salesforce, to support marketing performance tracking and funnel analysis.
Use GA4 and related digital analytics tools to understand acquisition behaviour, channel performance and conversion.
Partner closely with Data Engineering and technical teams to improve pipelines, models and reporting infrastructure.
Cross-functional collaboration and enablement
Act as a strategic partner to Marketing, Commercial and Product teams, embedding analytics into planning and execution.
Support campaign planning with data-driven targeting, segmentation and performance forecasting.
Define and track core marketing KPIs, north star metrics and success measures.
Champion a culture of data-driven decision making, continuously improving confidence in performance reporting and insights.



What Success Looks Like


Marketing and commercial teams have high confidence in performance data, attribution and reporting.
Clear visibility of what is driving acquisition and growth across all OakNorth products.
Strong adoption of dashboards and self-serve reporting across marketing and commercial teams.
Actionable insights consistently feeding into campaign planning, experimentation and strategy.
Effective collaboration with Product, Commercial and Data teams to support wider business goals.
Experience and knowledge
Proven experience in marketing analytics, commercial analytics or data analysis roles.
Experience working with marketing performance data, attribution models or experimentation frameworks.
Familiarity with CRM and marketing platforms such as HubSpot, Salesforce or similar.
Experience with data visualisation tools such as Looker, Tableau or similar.
Experience in fintech, financial services or B2B environments is beneficial but not essential.



Benefits & Perks


25 days holiday per year plus bank holidays, alongside enhanced family leave allowances.
Your choice of a high-quality laptop running macOS or Ubuntu.
Regular team and company socials, including informal breakfasts and dinners at OakNorth.
Access to cycle-to-work and electric vehicle schemes.

Strong experience working in agile or hybrid delivery models across distributed teams.
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and problem‑solving capabilities.

Title: Data analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst – Marketing & Commercial Performance

I'm looking for a Data Analyst to build and own a marketing and sales performance dashboard using Google BigQuery and Looker Studio.
This dashboard will support marketing, retail, and commercial teams with insights around campaign uplift, retailer investment impact, market share movement, and product halo effects across multiple markets.

Key Responsibilities
Build and deliver a Looker Studio dashboard underpinned by Google BigQuery
Combine sales, campaign activity, and market share data into a scalable reporting model
Define KPIs including uplift, baseline, market share, activity intensity, and halo effects
Measure campaign effectiveness and retailer investment impact
Analyse performance trends across products, retailers, and markets
Work closely with stakeholders to translate business questions into reporting logic
Present findings clearly and document models for handover
Requirements Essential
Strong experience as a Data or Insight Analyst in retail, e-commerce, marketing, or agency environments
Advanced SQL and strong Google BigQuery experience
Hands-on experience building dashboards in Looker Studio
Strong understanding of data modelling and reporting layer design
Experience analysing sales, campaign, and market share data
Ability to communicate insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders
Nice to Have
Python (pandas)
Wider GCP experience
Experience with GfK or similar syndicated market data
Background in retail, consumer, or e-commerce environments
Show more
